items.title: Two Britons self-isolating in UK after leaving hantavirus cruise ship
items.standfirst: A British man is among three people evacuated from the ship while two other Britons are self-isolating in the UK.
items.content: A British man is among three people evacuated from the ship while two other Britons are self-isolating in the UK.

items.title: Two Britons self-isolating after returning to UK from hantavirus-hit ship, say officials
items.standfirst: Pair left MV Hondius before outbreak was detected and are not reporting symptoms, according to UK Health Security AgencyTwo people who were onboard a cruise ship hit with a deadly outbreak of hantavirus have returned to the UK and are self-isolating, British health officials have said.Neither of the two British people, who left MV Hondius at St Helena in late April, are reporting symptoms, according to the UK Health Security Agency (UKSA). Continue reading...

clusters.title: Paris Saint-Germain returns to Champions League final after beating Bayern Munich
clusters.neutral_headline: Late Bayern pressure not enough as PSG return to Champions League final
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, BBC Top Stories, France24

items.title: Paris Saint-Germain returns to Champions League final after beating Bayern Munich
items.standfirst: The French league leader will face Arsenal in the final in Budapest, Hungary, on May 30, after the Gunners defeated Atletico Madrid 1-0 on Tuesday to progress 2-1 on aggregate.
items.content: The French league leader will face Arsenal in the final in Budapest, Hungary, on May 30, after the Gunners defeated Atletico Madrid 1-0 on Tuesday to progress 2-1 on aggregate.(Image credit: Martin Meissner)

items.title: Late Bayern pressure not enough as PSG return to Champions League final
items.standfirst: Harry Kane's injury-time equaliser earns Bayern Munich a 1-1 second leg draw at the Allianz Arena as holders PSG seal their return to the Champions League final 6-5 on aggregate, where they will meet Arsenal in Budapest.
items.content: Harry Kane's injury-time equaliser earns Bayern Munich a 1-1 second leg draw at the Allianz Arena as holders PSG seal their return to the Champions League final 6-5 on aggregate, where they will meet Arsenal in Budapest.

items.title: Champions League: Title-holders PSG edge Bayern Munich to reach second straight final
items.standfirst: The winners of the 2025 Champions League final will attempt to clinch the trophy a second time – on 30 May against Arsenal – after a 1-1 draw in their semi-final second leg against Bayern Munich on Wednesday sealed a 6-5 aggregate victory.
items.content: The winners of the 2025 Champions League final will attempt to clinch the trophy a second time – on 30 May against Arsenal – after a 1-1 draw in their semi-final second leg against Bayern Munich on Wednesday sealed a 6-5 aggregate victory.

clusters.title: Ted Turner, media mogul who revolutionised TV news by launching CNN, dies at 87
clusters.neutral_headline: Ted Turner, media mogul who revolutionised TV news by launching CNN, dies at 87
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Reuters Top News, Guardian Business

items.title: Ted Turner, media mogul who revolutionised TV news by launching CNN, dies at 87
items.standfirst: The media mogul pioneered the modern 24-hour news culture when he launched the Cable News Network in 1980.
items.content: The media mogul pioneered the modern 24-hour news culture when he launched the Cable News Network in 1980.

items.title: CNN founder Ted Turner, a pioneer of cable TV news, dies at 87
items.standfirst: Ted Turner, the media maverick and philanthropist who founded CNN, a pioneering 24-hour network that revolutionized television news, died peacefully Wednesday, surrounded by his family, according to a news release from Turner Enterprises. He was 87. [cnn.com]
items.content: Ted Turner, the media maverick and philanthropist who founded CNN, a pioneering 24-hour network that revolutionized television news, died peacefully Wednesday, surrounded by his family, according to a news release from Turner Enterprises. He was 87. [cnn.com]

items.title: Ted Turner, CNN founder who reshaped global news, dies aged 87
items.standfirst: The death of the media mogul who transformed TV news was confirmed by his family and reported by CNNTed Turner, the media mogul who founded CNN, has died aged 87, CNN reported on Wednesday, citing a news release from Turner Enterprises.In a statement, Mark Thompson, the chair and CEO of CNN Worldwide, said that “Ted was an intensely involved and committed leader, intrepid, fearless, and always willing to back a hunch and trust his own judgment.” Continue reading...
items.content: The death of the media mogul who transformed TV news was confirmed by his family and reported by CNNTed Turner, the media mogul who founded CNN, has died aged 87, CNN reported on Wednesday, citing a news release from Turner Enterprises.In a statement, Mark Thompson, the chair and CEO of CNN Worldwide, said that “Ted was an intensely involved and committed leader, intrepid, fearless, and always willing to back a hunch and trust his own judgment.” Continue reading...

clusters.title: Hantavirus on the rise in Argentina, where MV Hondius cruise ship set sail
clusters.neutral_headline: Hantavirus on the rise in Argentina, where MV Hondius cruise ship set sail
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: Hantavirus on the rise in Argentina, where MV Hondius cruise ship set sail
items.standfirst: Experts say that a surge of hantavirus cases in Argentina, where the MV Hondius cruise ship set sail, is in part due to climate change. The World Health Organization ranks the South American country as having the highest incidence of the rodent-borne disease.
items.content: Experts say that a surge of hantavirus cases in Argentina, where the MV Hondius cruise ship set sail, is in part due to climate change. The World Health Organization ranks the South American country as having the highest incidence of the rodent-borne disease.

items.title: Argentina races to find origins of cruise ship hantavirus outbreak, amid reports some passengers have returned to US
items.standfirst: Argentina, where the MV Hondius cruise departed, consistently ranked by WHO as having highest incidence of hantavirus in regionOfficials and experts in Argentina are scrambling to determine if their country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has gripped an Atlantic cruise ship, amid reports that a number of passengers have already returned to their home countries.Argentina, where the cruise to Antarctica departed, is consistently ranked by the World Health Organization (WHO) as having the highest incidence of the rare, rodent-borne disease in Latin America. Investigators there are working to contact trace the source of contamination. Continue reading...
items.content: Argentina, where the MV Hondius cruise departed, consistently ranked by WHO as having highest incidence of hantavirus in regionOfficials and experts in Argentina are scrambling to determine if their country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has gripped an Atlantic cruise ship, amid reports that a number of passengers have already returned to their home countries.Argentina, where the cruise to Antarctica departed, is consistently ranked by the World Health Organization (WHO) as having the highest incidence of the rare, rodent-borne disease in Latin America. Investigators there are working to contact trace the source of contamination. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump would fire Howard Lutnick if he saw video of his Epstein testimony, say House oversight committee Democrats – as it happened
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump would fire Howard Lutnick if he saw video of his Epstein testimony, say House...
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Trump would fire Howard Lutnick if he saw video of his Epstein testimony, say House oversight committee Democrats – as it happened
items.standfirst: This live blog is now closed.Howard Lutnick answers questions from US House over Jeffrey Epstein tiesSign up for the Breaking News US emailIt’s worth noting, ahead of Howard Lutnick’s closed-door testimony before House oversight committee members today, that the commerce secretary has refused to answer questions about his ties to Jeffrey Epstein from lawmakers during congressional hearings on Capitol Hill over the last month.In April, Represenative Madeleine Dean, a Democrat from Pennsylvania, asked whether the president expressed “concerns” about the commerce secretary’s relationship with Epstein. Lutnick refused to comment. Continue reading...
items.content: This live blog is now closed.Howard Lutnick answers questions from US House over Jeffrey Epstein tiesSign up for the Breaking News US emailIt’s worth noting, ahead of Howard Lutnick’s closed-door testimony before House oversight committee members today, that the commerce secretary has refused to answer questions about his ties to Jeffrey Epstein from lawmakers during congressional hearings on Capitol Hill over the last month.In April, Represenative Madeleine Dean, a Democrat from Pennsylvania, asked whether the president expressed “concerns” about the commerce secretary’s relationship with Epstein. Lutnick refused to comment. Continue reading...

items.title: Ro Khanna says Trump "would have fired" Howard Lutnick if he'd seen his testimony on Epstein
items.standfirst: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick testified Wednesday before the House Oversight Committee about the Jeffrey Epstein investigation. California Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna called the testimony "embarrassing." CBS News' Taurean Small has the latest.
items.content: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick testified Wednesday before the House Oversight Committee about the Jeffrey Epstein investigation. California Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna called the testimony "embarrassing." CBS News' Taurean Small has the latest.

clusters.title: US says migration has made Europe an ‘incubator’ for terrorism in new counter-terrorism strategy
clusters.neutral_headline: New Trump counterterrorism strategy brands Europe an 'incubator' for terrorism
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, France24

items.title: US says migration has made Europe an ‘incubator’ for terrorism in new counter-terrorism strategy
items.standfirst: The 16-page report was led by Trump-ally Sebastian Gorka, and places drug cartels in the Americas at the centre of counter-terrorism effortsThe Trump administration has accused Europe of being an “incubator” for terrorism fuelled by mass migration, in a new counter-terrorism strategy unveiled on Wednesday.The strategy also focuses on rooting out “violent left-wing extremists” including “radically pro-transgender” groups, as Trump’s conservative administration steps up its political attacks on opponents. Continue reading...
items.content: The 16-page report was led by Trump-ally Sebastian Gorka, and places drug cartels in the Americas at the centre of counter-terrorism effortsThe Trump administration has accused Europe of being an “incubator” for terrorism fuelled by mass migration, in a new counter-terrorism strategy unveiled on Wednesday.The strategy also focuses on rooting out “violent left-wing extremists” including “radically pro-transgender” groups, as Trump’s conservative administration steps up its political attacks on opponents. Continue reading...

items.title: New Trump counterterrorism strategy brands Europe an 'incubator' for terrorism
items.standfirst: The Trump administration on Wednesday unveiled a new counterterrorism strategy, accusing Europe of fostering terrorism through mass migration, while also expanding US domestic focus to include what it called “violent left-wing extremists” and “radically pro-transgender” groups.
items.content: The Trump administration on Wednesday unveiled a new counterterrorism strategy, accusing Europe of fostering terrorism through mass migration, while also expanding US domestic focus to include what it called “violent left-wing extremists” and “radically pro-transgender” groups.

clusters.title: Trump’s abrupt U-turn on plan to reopen Strait of Hormuz came after backlash from allies
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump pauses Hormuz plan 50 hours after he announced it - what happened
bullet_summary: The US president pauses the military operation to get ships through the Strait of Hormuz two days after he announced it
The US president said the operation has been paused after starting on Monday

items.title: Trump’s abrupt U-turn on plan to reopen Strait of Hormuz came after backlash from allies
items.standfirst: WASHINGTON — President Donald Trump’s abrupt reversal on his plan to help ships go through the Strait of Hormuz came after a key Gulf ally suspended the U.S. military’s ability to use its bases and airspace to carry out the operation, according to two U.S. officials.
items.content: WASHINGTON — President Donald Trump’s abrupt reversal on his plan to help ships go through the Strait of Hormuz came after a key Gulf ally suspended the U.S. military’s ability to use its bases and airspace to carry out the operation, according to two U.S. officials.
